mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near cycle highs above 4% continues to raise the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, exerting persistent downward pressure on valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month whenever FEDFUNDS remains elevated with no easing trend.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX posts positive mean 20d returns during periods with FEDFUNDS > 4% at a rate statistically indistinguishable from periods with FEDFUNDS < 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of 0.3 percentage points or more from its trailing 3-month low (a Sahm-rule-like trigger) signals an accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes broader earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 3-month minimum
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where the 0.3pp UNRATE trigger fires, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive, or the triggered-sample mean return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth alongside a rising unemployment rate jointly indicates a genuine labor market inflection rather than noise, since payrolls alone can be revised or noisy; the combined signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month, while either series alone crossing a threshold without the other is a weaker or false signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND UNRATE up >= 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: The joint-trigger sample shows no statistically significant difference in mean 20d forward returns versus the sample where only one of the two conditions holds, or Rank IC for the interaction term is below 0.03 across any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) trending upward act as a leading indicator of consumer and business confidence in demand, but this positive relationship to forward S&P 500 returns only holds when the Federal Reserve is in an easing or accommodative posture; during monetary tightening, rising housing starts instead reflect lagged effects and do not predict equity gains.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS flat or declining and HOUST trigger active, if mean 60d forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Short-horizon momentum in industrial production reflects real-time factory-floor conditions that are quickly priced by equity markets; a positive month-over-month change in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week due to immediate sentiment carryover into risk assets.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign hit-rate is not above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals resilient consumer spending, which supports corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month, reflecting the consumption-driven share of GDP and its direct link to earnings.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS trailing 3-month growth and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window, or the correlation sign flips persistently for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: The real policy rate, approximated by the gap between FEDFUNDS and trailing CPIAUCSL inflation, drives forward equity valuations: when nominal rates are high but inflation is falling faster, the resulting rise in real rates tightens financial conditions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, an effect not captured by either series alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12mo CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above 1.5%
failure_condition: When the real-rate trigger is active, if mean 60d forward SPX returns are not significantly negative versus baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC for the interaction term is below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL above a threshold rekindles inflation fears and expectations of renewed Fed tightening, causing an immediate risk-off reaction and negative S&P 500 returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: In more than half of historical instances where the CPI acceleration trigger fires, SPX 5-day forward returns are non-negative, or the triggered-sample mean return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean at p > 0.05.
